reason: The statement applies because Elijah, a prophet of the LORD, warns Ahaziah, the king of Samaria, of his impending death as a consequence of seeking guidance from Baalzebub, a pagan god. Elijah delivers the message from the LORD that Ahaziah will not recover from his sickness but will surely die as a judgment for seeking help from a false god. This can be seen as a form of warning the Israelites or Sons of Israel of a destructive outcome as a result of turning away from the true God.
